      id_bucket == "no_ID" ~ delta_no_ID,
      id_bucket == "Single_ID" ~ delta_Single_ID,
      id_bucket == "Multiple_IDs" ~ delta_Multiple_IDs
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(group_by_type) %>%
  arrange(id_bucket, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(label_y = cumsum(count) - count / 2) %>%
  ungroup()

p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta <- ggplot(stacked_long_version_delta, aes(x = group_by_version, y = count, fill = id_bucket)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = label_y,
      label = ifelse(count == 0 & delta_value == 0, "", paste0(count, "\nΔ", sprintf("%+d", delta_value)))
    ),
    color = "black",
    size = 2.5,
    lineheight = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, labels = compact_bar_labels) +
  labs(
    title = "ID Summary by Data Version and ID Type (with Δ vs Original)",
    x = "Data Version and ID Type",
    y = "Feature Count",
    fill = "ID Count Class"
  ) +
  version_group_annotation +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  bar_plot_theme

p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta <- ggplot(id_summary_with_delta_df, aes(x = group_by_version, y = Total_IDs, fill = id_type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = pmax(Total_IDs - 8, 0),
      label = ifelse(Total_IDs == 0 & delta_Total_IDs == 0, "", paste0(Total_IDs, "\nΔ", sprintf("%+d", delta_Total_IDs)))
    ),
    color = "black",
    size = 2.6,
    fontface = "bold",
    lineheight = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = id_type_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, labels = compact_bar_labels) +
  labs(
    title = "Total IDs by Data Version and ID Type (with Δ vs Original)",
    x = "Data Version and ID Type",
    y = "Total_IDs"
  ) +
  version_group_annotation +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  bar_plot_theme

p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta <- ggplot(stacked_long_type_delta, aes(x = group_by_type, y = count, fill = id_bucket)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = label_y,
      label = ifelse(count == 0 & delta_value == 0, "", paste0(count, "\nΔ", sprintf("%+d", delta_value)))
    ),
    color = "black",
    size = 2.5,
    lineheight = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, labels = compact_bar_labels) +
  labs(
    title = "ID Summary by ID Type and Data Version (with Δ vs Original)",
    x = "ID Type and Data Version",
    y = "Feature Count",
    fill = "ID Count Class"
  ) +
  type_group_annotation +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  bar_plot_theme

p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta <- ggplot(id_summary_with_delta_df, aes(x = group_by_type, y = Total_IDs, fill = id_type)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(
    aes(
      y = pmax(Total_IDs - 8, 0),
      label = ifelse(Total_IDs == 0 & delta_Total_IDs == 0, "", paste0(Total_IDs, "\nΔ", sprintf("%+d", delta_Total_IDs)))
    ),
    color = "black",
    size = 2.6,
    fontface = "bold",
    lineheight = 0.9
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = id_type_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, labels = compact_bar_labels) +
  labs(
    title = "Total IDs by ID Type and Data Version (with Δ vs Original)",
    x = "ID Type and Data Version",
    y = "Total_IDs"
  ) +
  type_group_annotation +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  bar_plot_theme

print(p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta)

print(p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta)

print(p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta)

print(p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta)


ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_stacked_by_data_version_with_delta.png",
  plot = p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_stacked_by_data_version_with_delta.svg",
  plot = p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version_with_delta.png",
  plot = p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version_with_delta.svg",
  plot = p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_stacked_by_id_type_with_delta.png",
  plot = p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_stacked_by_id_type_with_delta.svg",
  plot = p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type_with_delta.png",
  plot = p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type_with_delta.svg",
  plot = p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta,
  width = 16,
  height = 8
)
## delta_df visualizations (baseline = Original)

baseline_steps <- tibble::tribble(
  ~from_version, ~to_version, ~step_label,
  "Original", "Cleaned", "Original -> Cleaned",
  "Original", "Translated", "Original -> Translated",
  "Original", "Traverse", "Original -> Traverse",
  "Original", "addEquivalent", "Original -> addEquivalent"
)

delta_plot_df <- delta_df %>%
  mutate(parts = stringr::str_split(row_name, "_", simplify = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(
    id_type = parts[, 2],
    from_version = parts[, 3],
    to_id_type = parts[, 4],
    to_version = parts[, 5]
  ) %>%
  select(-parts) %>%
  filter(
    id_type %in% id_types,
    from_version %in% data_versions,
    to_version %in% data_versions
  ) %>%
  left_join(baseline_steps, by = c("from_version", "to_version")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(step_label)) %>%
  mutate(
    id_type = factor(id_type, levels = id_types),
    step_label = factor(step_label, levels = baseline_steps$step_label)
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(c(no_ID, Single_ID, Multiple_IDs, Total_IDs), ~ tidyr::replace_na(., 0)))

## Plot 1: Total_IDs deltas vs Original (line plot)
delta_total_df <- delta_plot_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Total_IDs))

p_delta_total_vs_original <- ggplot(delta_total_df, aes(x = step_label, y = Total_IDs, color = id_type, group = id_type)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 2.4) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = Total_IDs),
    vjust = ifelse(delta_total_df$Total_IDs >= 0, -0.55, 1.25),
    size = 2.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Delta Total_IDs vs Original",
    x = "Comparison",
    y = "Delta Total_IDs",
    color = "ID Type"
  ) +
  plot_theme

## Plot 2: no_ID / Single_ID / Multiple_IDs deltas vs Original (line plot)
delta_comp_long <- delta_plot_df %>%
  select(id_type, step_label, no_ID, Single_ID, Multiple_IDs) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(no_ID, Single_ID, Multiple_IDs),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(delta)) %>%
  mutate(metric = factor(metric, levels = c("no_ID", "Single_ID", "Multiple_IDs")))

p_delta_comp_vs_original <- ggplot(delta_comp_long, aes(x = step_label, y = delta, color = metric, group = metric)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(delta == 0, "", delta)),
    vjust = ifelse(delta_comp_long$delta >= 0, -0.45, 1.15),
    size = 2.3,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ id_type, ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Delta Composition vs Original",
    x = "Comparison",
    y = "Delta Count",
    color = "Metric"
  ) +
  plot_theme

print(p_delta_total_vs_original)

print(p_delta_comp_vs_original)


ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/delta_total_vs_original.png",
  plot = p_delta_total_vs_original,
  width = 12,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/delta_total_vs_original.svg",
  plot = p_delta_total_vs_original,
  width = 12,
  height = 7
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/delta_composition_vs_original.png",
  plot = p_delta_comp_vs_original,
  width = 14,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

ggsave(
  filename = "MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots/delta_composition_vs_original.svg",
  plot = p_delta_comp_vs_original,
  width = 14,
  height = 8
)
## Sized barplots using MetaProViz internal sizing helpers
## (kept separate so existing plotting code remains untouched)
if (!exists("p_id_stacked_by_version") ||
    !exists("p_total_ids_by_version") ||
    !exists("p_id_stacked_by_type") ||
    !exists("p_total_ids_by_type") ||
    !exists("p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta") ||
    !exists("p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta") ||
    !exists("p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta") ||
    !exists("p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta")) {
  stop("Expected barplot objects were not found. Run the previous plotting chunk first.")
}

if (!("plot_grob_superplot" %in% getNamespaceExports("MetaProViz") ||
      exists("plot_grob_superplot", where = asNamespace("MetaProViz"), inherits = FALSE))) {
  stop("MetaProViz:::plot_grob_superplot is not available in this session.")
}

clean_plot_for_sizing <- function(plot_obj) {
  is_group_annotation_layer <- function(layer) {
    is_text <- inherits(layer$geom, "GeomText")
    has_own_data <- isFALSE(layer$inherit.aes)
    has_data <- !is.null(layer$data) && is.data.frame(layer$data)
    has_group_cols <- has_data && any(c("data_version", "id_type") %in% names(layer$data))
    is_text && has_own_data && has_group_cols
  }

  keep_layer <- vapply(plot_obj$layers, function(layer) !is_group_annotation_layer(layer), logical(1))
  plot_obj$layers <- plot_obj$layers[keep_layer]

  # Remove off-panel rendering side-effects added for manual group labels.
  plot_obj <- plot_obj +
    ggplot2::coord_cartesian(clip = "on") +
    ggplot2::theme(plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 5.5))

  plot_obj
}

size_bar_plot <- function(plot_obj, plot_name, out_base) {
  x_var <- rlang::as_name(plot_obj$mapping$x)
  if (is.null(plot_obj$data) || !(x_var %in% names(plot_obj$data))) {
    stop("Could not infer x-axis variable from plot data for: ", plot_name)
  }

  x_values <- plot_obj$data[[x_var]]
  x_levels <- if (is.factor(x_values)) levels(x_values) else unique(as.character(x_values))
  x_levels <- x_levels[!is.na(x_levels) & x_levels != ""]

  if (length(x_levels) == 0) {
    stop("No x-axis levels extracted for sizing helper in: ", plot_name)
  }

  # Use short synthetic condition labels for sizing internals.
  # This preserves count-driven width while preventing oversized axis-b spacing.
  metadata_sample <- tibble::tibble(
    Conditions = sprintf("c%03d", seq_along(x_levels))
  )

  plot_for_sizing <- clean_plot_for_sizing(plot_obj)

  plot_sized_gtable <- MetaProViz:::plot_grob_superplot(
    input_plot = plot_for_sizing,
    metadata_info = NULL,
    metadata_sample = metadata_sample,
    subtitle = NULL,
    plot_name = plot_name,
    plot_type = "Bar"
  )

  width_cm <- sum(grid::convertUnit(plot_sized_gtable$width, "cm", valueOnly = TRUE))
  height_cm <- sum(grid::convertUnit(plot_sized_gtable$height, "cm", valueOnly = TRUE))

  # Add safety padding to avoid clipping at top/right edges on export devices.
  width_cm_padded <- width_cm + max(0.8, width_cm * 0.06)
  height_cm_padded <- height_cm + max(1.2, height_cm * 0.12)

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = file.path("MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots", paste0(out_base, "_sized.png")),
    plot = plot_sized_gtable,
    width = width_cm_padded,
    height = height_cm_padded,
    units = "cm",
    dpi = 300
  )

  ggplot2::ggsave(
    filename = file.path("MetaProViz_Results/PK/id_summary_plots", paste0(out_base, "_sized.svg")),
    plot = plot_sized_gtable,
    width = width_cm_padded,
    height = height_cm_padded,
    units = "cm"
  )

  invisible(list(
    Plot_Sized = plot_sized_gtable,
    Plot_Sized_gtable = plot_sized_gtable,
    width_cm = width_cm,
    height_cm = height_cm,
    width_cm_padded = width_cm_padded,
    height_cm_padded = height_cm_padded
  ))
}

sized_barplots <- list(
  id_summary_stacked_by_data_version = size_bar_plot(
    p_id_stacked_by_version,
    plot_name = "ID Summary by Data Version and ID Type",
    out_base = "id_summary_stacked_by_data_version"
  ),
  id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version = size_bar_plot(
    p_total_ids_by_version,
    plot_name = "Total IDs by Data Version and ID Type",
    out_base = "id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version"
  ),
  id_summary_stacked_by_id_type = size_bar_plot(
    p_id_stacked_by_type,
    plot_name = "ID Summary by ID Type and Data Version",
    out_base = "id_summary_stacked_by_id_type"
  ),
  id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type = size_bar_plot(
    p_total_ids_by_type,
    plot_name = "Total IDs by ID Type and Data Version",
    out_base = "id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type"
  ),
  id_summary_stacked_by_data_version_with_delta = size_bar_plot(
    p_id_stacked_by_version_with_delta,
    plot_name = "ID Summary by Data Version and ID Type (with Δ vs Original)",
    out_base = "id_summary_stacked_by_data_version_with_delta"
  ),
  id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version_with_delta = size_bar_plot(
    p_total_ids_by_version_with_delta,
    plot_name = "Total IDs by Data Version and ID Type (with Δ vs Original)",
    out_base = "id_summary_total_ids_by_data_version_with_delta"
  ),
  id_summary_stacked_by_id_type_with_delta = size_bar_plot(
    p_id_stacked_by_type_with_delta,
    plot_name = "ID Summary by ID Type and Data Version (with Δ vs Original)",
    out_base = "id_summary_stacked_by_id_type_with_delta"
  ),
  id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type_with_delta = size_bar_plot(
    p_total_ids_by_type_with_delta,
    plot_name = "Total IDs by ID Type and Data Version (with Δ vs Original)",
    out_base = "id_summary_total_ids_by_id_type_with_delta"
  )
)
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.
#> Coordinate system already present.
#> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one.

3. Integrate feature IDs with prior knowledge

Update below (is from revision branch). Here we also need to create a summary table according to reviewers request!

Given that in some cases we have multiple KEGG IDs for a measured feature, we will check if this causes mapping to multiple, different entries in the KEGG pathway-metabolite sets:

#Load Kegg pathways:
KEGG_Pathways <- metsigdb_kegg()

## from the latest "MetaboliteIDs_addEquivalent", build the input_df for checkmatch_pk_to_data
Tissue_MetaData_Extended <- 
    as.data.frame(
        MetaboliteIDs_addEquivalent
    ) %>%
    select(
        Metabolite,
        KEGG,
        manual_correction,
        correction_tbl
    )

#check mapping with metadata
ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways <- MetaProViz::checkmatch_pk_to_data(data = Tissue_MetaData_Extended,
                                                           input_pk = KEGG_Pathways,
                                                           metadata_info = c(InputID = "KEGG", PriorID = "MetaboliteID", grouping_variable = "term"))
#> Warning in MetaProViz::checkmatch_pk_to_data(data = Tissue_MetaData_Extended, :
#> 251 NA values were removed from column KEGG
#> Warning in MetaProViz::checkmatch_pk_to_data(data = Tissue_MetaData_Extended, :
#> 7 duplicated IDs were removed from column KEGG
#> data has multiple IDs per measurement = TRUE. input_pk has multiple IDs per entry = FALSE.
#> data has 319 unique entries with 357 unique KEGG IDs. Of those IDs, 256 match, which is 71.7086834733894%.
#> input_pk has 6525 unique entries with 6525 unique MetaboliteID IDs. Of those IDs, 256 are detected in the data, which is 3.92337164750958%.
#> Warning in MetaProViz::checkmatch_pk_to_data(data = Tissue_MetaData_Extended, :
#> There are cases where multiple detected IDs match to multiple prior knowledge
#> IDs of the same category
# console out >>
# data has multiple IDs per measurement = TRUE. input_pk has multiple IDs per entry = FALSE.
# data has 319 unique entries with 357 unique KEGG IDs. Of those IDs, 256 match, which is 71.7086834733894%.
# input_pk has 6586 unique entries with 6586 unique MetaboliteID IDs. Of those IDs, 256 are detected in the data, which is 3.88703310051625%.
# [2026-04-08 09:55:51] [WARN]    [MetaProViz] There are cases where multiple detected IDs match to multiple prior knowledge IDs of the same category

# inspect results
ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary <- as.data.frame(ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways$data_summary)
ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_GroupingVariable_summary <- as.data.frame(ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways$GroupingVariable_summary)


# There are some different features which share the same and ONLY KEGG ID.
# Take Tissue_MetaData_Extended KEGG and Metabolite columns, left_join the 
# ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary results on there


# merge the Metabolite name back into data_summary, because it was lost during checkmatch_pk_to_data
ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary <- 
    Tissue_MetaData_Extended %>%
        select(
            Metabolite,
            KEGG,
            manual_correction,
            correction_tbl
        ) %>%
        left_join(
            ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary,
            by = "KEGG"
        )


if (!dir.exists("MetaProViz_Results/PK/CheckMatchID_Detected-to-PK")) {
  dir.create("MetaProViz_Results/PK/CheckMatchID_Detected-to-PK", recursive = TRUE)
}

# convert lists inside ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary to strings
ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary[] <- lapply(
  ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary,
  function(x) {
    if (is.list(x)) sapply(x, toString) else x
  }
)
## save checkmatch results
write.csv2(
    ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary,
    "MetaProViz_Results/PK/CheckMatchID_Detected-to-PK/ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary_manual_save.csv"
)

write.csv2(
    ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_GroupingVariable_summary,
    "MetaProViz_Results/PK/CheckMatchID_Detected-to-PK/ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_GroupingVariable_summary_manual_save.csv"
)

problems_terms <- ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_GroupingVariable_summary %>%
  filter(!Group_Conflict_Notes== "None")
Terms in KEGG pathways where the same measured feature mapps with more than one ID, which will inflate the enrichment analysis.

C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00031 Amino sugar and nucleotide sugar metabolism

C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00031 Biosynthesis of secondary metabolites
C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00267 Microbial metabolism in diverse environments
C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00221 Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis
C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00221 Metabolic pathways
C00221, C00031, C00267 3 3 C00031 Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis
C00258, C01921 2 2 C01921 Metabolic pathways
C00258, C01921 2 2 C00258 Metabolic pathways
C00309, C00508 2 2 C00508 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00309, C00508 2 2 C00309 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00309, C00508 2 2 C00508 Metabolic pathways
C00309, C00508 2 2 C00309 Metabolic pathways
C00379, C01904 2 2 C00379 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00379, C01904 2 2 C01904 Metabolic pathways
C00379, C01904 2 2 C01904 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00379, C01904 2 2 C00379 Metabolic pathways
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C00532 Metabolic pathways
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C00474 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C00532 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C00474 Metabolic pathways
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C01904 Metabolic pathways
C00474, C00532, C01904 3 3 C01904 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00502, C05411 2 2 C00502 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C00502, C05411 2 2 C05411 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions
C01087, C02630 2 2 C01087 Metabolic pathways
C01087, C02630 2 2 C02630 Metabolic pathways
C03242, C16522 2 2 C16522 Biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids
C03242, C16522 2 2 C03242 Biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids
C03460, C03722 2 2 C03460 Metabolic pathways
C03460, C03722 2 2 C03722 Metabolic pathways
C05793, C05794 2 2 C05793 Porphyrin metabolism
C05793, C05794 2 2 C05794 Porphyrin metabolism
C17737, C00695 2 2 C00695 Secondary bile acid biosynthesis
C17737, C00695 2 2 C17737 Secondary bile acid biosynthesis


Dependent on the biological question and the organism and prior knowledge, one can either maintain the metabolite ID of the more likely metabolite (e.g. in human its more likely that we have L-aminoacid than D-aminoacid) or the metabolite ID that is represented in more/less pathways (specificity). If we are looking into the cases where we do have multiple IDs, for cases with no match to the prior knowledge we can just maintain one ID, whilst for cases with exactly one match we should maintain the ID that is found in the prior knowledge.

# Select the cases where a feature has multiple IDs
multipleIDs <- ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary %>%
  filter(original_count>1)
Terms in KEGG pathways where the same measured feature mapps with more than one ID, which may inflate the enrichment analysis.
KEGG InputID_select original_count matches_count matches Group_Conflict_Notes ActionRequired Action_Specific
C00583, C02912 C00583 2 1 C00583 None None None
C05793, C05794 NA 2 2 C05793, C05794 Porphyrin metabolism Check KeepOneID
C00140, C03878 C00140 2 1 C00140 None None None
C00270, C19910 C00270 2 1 C00270 None None None
C00474, C00532, C01904 NA 3 3 C00474, C00532, C01904 Metabolic pathways || Pentose and glucuronate interconversions || None Check KeepOneID
C00072, C01041 NA 2 2 C00072, C01041 Metabolic pathways || None || Ascorbate and aldarate metabolism Check KeepOneID
C00180, C00539 C00180 2 1 C00180 None None None
C17737, C00695 NA 2 2 C17737, C00695 None || Secondary bile acid biosynthesis Check KeepOneID
C00491, C01420 C00491 2 1 C00491 None None None
C03242, C16522 NA 2 2 C03242, C16522 Biosynthesis of unsaturated fatty acids || None Check KeepOneID
C00095, C02336 C00095 2 1 C00095 None None None
C00221, C00031, C00267 NA 3 3 C00221, C00031, C00267 Microbial metabolism in diverse environments || None || Galactose metabolism || Metabolic pathways || Pentose phosphate pathway || Carbon metabolism || Amino sugar and nucleotide sugar metabolism || Glycolysis / Gluconeogenesis || Biosynthesis of secondary metabolites Check KeepOneID
C00092, C00668 NA 2 2 C00092, C00668 None || Biosynthesis of secondary metabolites || Metabolic pathways Check KeepOneID
C00025, C00217, C00302 NA 3 3 C00025, C00217, C00302 None || Metabolic pathways || D-Amino acid metabolism Check KeepOneID
C00258, C01921 NA 2 2 C00258, C01921 None || Metabolic pathways Check KeepOneID
C00093, C03189 C00093 2 1 C00093 None None None
C00160, C03547 NA 2 2 C00160, C03547 None Check KeepEachID
C01040, C05410 C01040 2 1 C01040 None None None
C00155, C05330 C00155 2 1 C00155 None None None
C00208, C00897 C00208 2 1 C00208 None None None
C02052, C00464 C00464 2 1 C00464 None None None
C01835, C00721, C00420 NA 3 2 C01835, C00721 None Check KeepEachID
C00159, C00936 C00159 2 1 C00159 None None None
C00077, C00515 NA 2 2 C00077, C00515 D-Amino acid metabolism || None || Biosynthesis of various other secondary metabolites || Biosynthesis of secondary metabolites || Metabolic pathways Check KeepOneID
C04148, C05597 C04148 2 1 C04148 None None None
C03460, C03722 NA 2 2 C03460, C03722 None || Metabolic pathways Check KeepOneID
C00309, C00508 NA 2 2 C00309, C00508 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions || Metabolic pathways Check KeepOneID
C00065, C00716, C00740 NA 3 3 C00065, C00716, C00740 None || D-Amino acid metabolism || Vancomycin resistance || Metabolic pathways || Glycine, serine and threonine metabolism Check KeepOneID
C01096, C02810 C01096 2 1 C01096 None None None
C01087, C02630 NA 2 2 C01087, C02630 Metabolic pathways || None Check KeepOneID
C00379, C01904 NA 2 2 C00379, C01904 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions || Metabolic pathways || None Check KeepOneID
C00502, C05411 NA 2 2 C00502, C05411 Pentose and glucuronate interconversions || None Check KeepOneID
C00671, C06008 C00671 2 1 C00671 None None None
C01991, C00989 C00989 2 1 C00989 None None None
C03079, C05947 C05947 2 1 C05947 None None None


In case of ActionRequired=="Check", we can look into the column Action_Specific which contains additional information. In case of the entry KeepEachID, multiple matches to the prior knowledge were found, yet the features are in different pathways (=GroupingVariable). Yet, in case of KeepOneID, the different IDs map to the same pathway in the prior knowledge for at least one case and therefore keeping both would inflate the enrichment analysis.

SelectedIDs <- ccRCC_to_KEGGPathways_data_summary %>%
  #Expand rows where Action == KeepEachID by splitting `matches`
  mutate(matches_split = if_else(Action_Specific == "KeepEachID", matches, NA_character_)) %>%
  # separate_rows(matches_split, sep = ",\\s*") %>%
  mutate(
      InputID_select = if_else(
          Action_Specific  == "KeepEachID", 
          matches_split, 
          InputID_select
      )
  ) %>%
  select(-matches_split) %>%
  mutate(  # remember IDs before to compare at the end of this pipe chain
      InputID_before = InputID_select
  ) %>%
  #Select one ID for AcionSpecific==KeepOneID
  dplyr::mutate(
      InputID_select = case_when(
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00025, C00217, C00302" ~ "C00025", # L-Glutamate vs D-Glutamate vs Glutamate compounds. Keep L-Glutamate, most prevalent in KEGG pathways
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00065, C00716, C00740" ~ "C00065", # L-Serine vs Serine vs D-Serine
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00072, C01041"         ~ "C00072", # Ascorbate vs Monodehydroascorbate. almost same molecule, just one H molecule more in Ascorbate. Keep C00072 since in more pathways
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00077, C00515"         ~ "C00077", # L-Ornithine vs D-Ornithine. Keep L-Ornithine
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00092, C00668"         ~ "C00092, C00668", # D-Glucose 6-phosphate vs alpha-D-Glucose 6-phosphate. Both present in lots of KEGG_Pathways, with a very low intersection of pathway terms between them. 2 shared pathways and 16 non-shared ones. Remove one of the KEGG IDs from pathways which include both
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00221, C00031, C00267" ~ "C00031", # These are D- and L-Glucose and alpha-D-Glucose. We have human samples, so in this conflict we will maintain L-Glucose
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00258, C01921"         ~ "C00258", # D-Glycerate vs Glycocholate. This is an ID mismatch, since they are clearly very different molecules. C01921 was added at some translation step, discard it
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00309, C00508"         ~ "C00309", # D-Ribulose vs L-Ribulose. Keep C00309, since both map to the same two pathways, so it is irrelevant
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00379, C01904"         ~ "C00379", # Xylitol vs D-Arabitol/D-Lyxitol. Stereochemic difference only.  C00379 maps to one more pathway
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00474, C00532, C01904" ~ "C00474", # Ribitol/Adonitol vs L-Arabitol vs D-Arabitol. C00474 maps to one more pathway
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C00502, C05411"         ~ "C05411", # D-Xylonate vs L-Xylonate. Both map to two pathways, one overlapping. We keep L 
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C01087, C02630"         ~ "C01087, C02630", # (R)-2-Hydroxyglutarate vs 2-Hydroxyglutarate. Both map to three pathways, one overlapping. --> Remove (R)-2-Hydroxyglutarate from KEGG pathway "Metabolic Pathways"
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C03242, C16522"         ~ "C03242", # Dihomo-gamma-linolenate vs Icosatrienoic acid. Both fatty acids with different double bonds. C03242 maps to two more pathways.
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C03460, C03722"         ~ "C03722", # 2-Methylprop-2-enoyl-CoA versus Quinolinate. No evidence, hence we keep the one present in more pathways (C03722=7 pathways, C03460=2 pathway)
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C05793, C05794"         ~ "C05793", # L-Urobilin vs Urobilin. C05793 was in originally assigned, keep it.
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" & matches ==  "C17737, C00695"         ~ "C00695", # Allocholic acid versus Cholic acid. No evidence, hence we keep the one present in more pathways (C00695 = 4 pathways, C17737 = 1 pathway)
          Action_Specific == "KeepOneID" ~ InputID_select,  # Keep NA where not matched manually
          TRUE ~ InputID_select
      ),
      corrected_during_checkmatch = is.na(InputID_before) ## flag whether changed above
  ) %>%
  select(  ## remove temp column
      -InputID_before
  )
  

## We identified that in some cases in our data, there are two MetaboliteIDs with e.g. stereochemical differences but they map to multiple pathways. 
## Therefore, we are going to remember them here and later remove them from the Pathway PK

# Remove (R)-2-Hydroxyglutarate (C01087) from KEGG pathway "Metabolic Pathways"
# C00092, C00668: remove C00668 from Pathways which contain both of them


Lastly, we need to add the column including our selected IDs to the metadata table:

Tissue_MetaData_Extended_cleaned <- 
  merge(
    x = SelectedIDs %>% 
        select(Metabolite, InputID_select, corrected_during_checkmatch),
    y = Tissue_MetaData_Extended,
    by = "Metabolite",
    all.y = TRUE
  ) %>%
  select(
      Metabolite, KEGG, InputID_select, manual_correction, correction_tbl, corrected_during_checkmatch
  )


if (!dir.exists("MetaProViz_Results/PK/End_Result_Fig2")) {
  dir.create("MetaProViz_Results/PK/End_Result_Fig2", recursive = TRUE)
}

write.csv2(
    Tissue_MetaData_Extended_cleaned,
    "MetaProViz_Results/PK/End_Result_Fig2/Tissue_MetaData_Extended_cleaned.csv"
)

This final table will be used as input for the enrichment analysis

#> [109] stats4_4.4.3                xfun_0.52                   graphlayouts_1.2.2          Biobase_2.66.0             
#> [113] statmod_1.5.1               factoextra_1.0.7            matrixStats_1.5.0           pheatmap_1.0.13
